message:
[medium] airtable: reject base_url overrides with query, fragment, or userinfo

validate_base_url_for_auth pinned the host to api.airtable.com in
direct-token mode and accepted any URL in credential_id mode, but
returned parsed.to_string() verbatim — query, fragment, and userinfo
components survived into AirtableClient's downstream
format!("{base_url}/{path}", ...) URL construction. A configure
payload like
    {"token": "patX", "base_url": "https://api.airtable.com/v0?leak=x"}
would pass validation and then leak `?leak=x` on every Airtable API
request (with endpoint path concatenated after the `?` boundary).
Userinfo (`https://attacker:pw@api.airtable.com/...`) would bake
into every request URL, silently overriding the PAT the connector
sets via the Authorization header.

- validate_base_url_for_auth now rejects userinfo (username or
  password) and query/fragment components before the host-allowlist /
  scheme-allowlist checks, in both Token and CredentialId modes.
- Six new unit tests: accept clean api.airtable.com (happy path),
  Token reject (query / fragment / userinfo), CredentialId reject
  (query), and a substring-smuggle regression verifying
  `https://evil.com/api.airtable.com/v0` is rejected because the
  parsed host is evil.com, not api.airtable.com.

Verification: `cargo check -p fcp-airtable` under CARGO_TARGET_DIR=
/tmp/fcp-review-p7 cannot complete because fcp-crypto fails to build
due to a committed indentation regression in crates/fcp-crypto/src/
hpke_seal.rs (line 366 closes the `hpke_sealed_box_bytes_roundtrip`
test fn body at 8-space indent instead of 4-space, unbalancing the
rest of the tests mod and triggering an unexpected closing delimiter
at line 828). That regression is in another pane's domain. Airtable
diff reviewed manually: additions are std url::Url accessor calls
returning standard shapes, with error shapes identical to the
surrounding validator branches. Variants `AirtableAuth::Token` and
`AirtableAuth::CredentialId` verified present at connector.rs:65-66.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
